rulings.Case Title: WENDY SHARP VS. A STAR HOLDINGS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; And Declaration Of Stephen M. Williams In Support Of Motion To Compel Respnses To Special And Form Interrogatories, Set One, To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set One And For Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery Calendar for April 23, 2025, Line 8. PETITIONER WENDY SHARP's Motion To Compel Responses To Special And Form Interrogatories, Set One, To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents Set One And For Monetary Sanction. Petitioner Wendy Sharp seeks an order compelling responses to collection-related discovery and for sanctions. Respondents oppose on the ground that proceedings in the trial court are stayed because they have perfected their appeal of the judgment and filed a supersedeas appeal bond. The court denies the motion. This court entered judgment on an arbitration award in favor of petitioner Sharp on January 31, 2025 in the amount of $306,112.86 against respondents Peter Acworth and A Star Holding LLC. Sharp concedes that respondents have filed a supersedeas appeal bond and perfected their appeal, thereby staying enforcement of a judgment or order. Sharp nonetheless contends that the appellate stay does not extend to discovery. This court disagrees where Sharp's discovery is in service of collecting her judgment. In any event an appeal bond serves that function and ensures that Sharp will receive her judgment if it is affirmed. (See CCP 917.1(b).) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Intervenor Cody Molicas Responses With Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery Calendar for April 23, 2025, Line 9. DEFENDANT PETER SUTER's Motion To Compel Intervenor Cody Molicas Responses With Proof Of Service. Defendant Peter Suter's unopposed motion to compel Intervenor Cody Molica's responses to Suter's Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, is granted. Molica failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2030.290(a); 2031.300(a).) Molica is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by responsive documents within 10 court days of entry of this order. Failure to comply with this discovery order may result in monetary sanctions or other sanctions. Suter does not seek sanctions on this motion and none are awarded. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: ANDREA ANDERSON VS. FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion / Discovery Calendar for April 23, 2025, Line 7. DEFENDANT FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION's MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS. Defendant Federal Express Corporation's motion for sanctions pursuant to CCP 128.7 is denied. Sanctions under 128.7 are statutory; they lie only where an attorney's implied certification of the matters listed in 128.7(b) are false or lack reasonable support. FedEx's sanctions request essentially makes the case that opposing counsel's pleadings are so frequently conclusory and lacking necessary ultimate facts that the court must conclude that these conclusory pleadings, including the complaint initially filed here, are "being presented primarily for an improper purpose, such as to harass or to cause unnecessary delay or needless increase in the cost of litigation." (CCP 128.7(b)(1).) The range in factual detail of pleadings filed in this court is so broad that the court cannot say based on conclusory pleadings alone that counsel's purpose must have been improper. The court also denies plaintiff Andrea Anderson's cross-motion for sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
